content: Intrinsic curiosity signals in AI reinforcement learning systems (ICM/RND) operate as an 'active encoding gate' for agent memory that mirrors the human brain's mechanism of prioritizing memory consolidation for curious, unexplored experiences over mundane predictable ones. This suggests current AI curiosity methods that reward prediction error alone can be improved by adopting the human brain's active memory selection mechanism that discards unrewarding surprising outliers while preserving structured, learnable novelty.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
